---
name: New template proposal
about: Propose a new visual template before building it
title: "[template] "
labels: ["template", "needs-triage"]
assignees: []
---

> **Open this issue first, get a thumbs-up from a maintainer, then build.**
> This prevents two contributors building the same idea in parallel.

## Template name

<!-- Short, lowercase, kebab-case. Examples: editorial-magazine, isometric-city, neon-cyberpunk. -->

## One-line pitch

<!-- "It's a ___ that feels like ___." Example: "A retro newsroom that feels like 1970s broadsheet." -->

## Inspiration

<!-- Drop links, mood boards, screenshots, references. The more concrete the better. -->

## Distinct from existing templates because…

<!-- We already have: terminal, brutalist, press, bento-os, spatial-walk, cinematic.
     Explain how this is meaningfully different from all six. -->

## Rough mock or wireframe

<!-- Attach a sketch, Figma frame, ASCII layout, or even a paragraph describing the layout.
     Doesn't need to be polished — it just needs to communicate the idea. -->

## Technical considerations

- [ ] Uses Three.js / WebGL?
- [ ] Uses scroll-jacking?
- [ ] Heavy media (videos / large images)?
- [ ] Needs new fonts beyond what's already loaded?
- [ ] Other: 

## Data sections you'll render

<!-- Templates must render every section of LayoutData. Check off your plan: -->
- [ ] Identity (name, headline, bio)
- [ ] Experience
- [ ] Skills
- [ ] Platforms (GitHub, LeetCode, Codeforces, Dev.to, Hugging Face)
- [ ] Projects (featured + others, with modal)
- [ ] Files (resume + custom files)
- [ ] Custom links + socials
- [ ] Education

## Willing to build?

- [x] Yes — I'll open the PR once I have maintainer ack
- [ ] No — putting the idea out there for someone else to pick up
